slides.title: Vue 3 Composition API & Reactivity
slides.content: The Composition API introduces a highly flexible and reusable way to organize component logic in Vue 3, replacing the traditional Options API with functions (`ref`, `reactive`, `computed`, `watch`). This modular approach allows developers to group logic by functional feature rather than by component property (like 'data', 'methods', 'computed'). By utilizing `ref` for reactive primitives and `reactive` for deep reactive objects, Vue 3 ensures that UI components remain efficiently reactive in response to underlying data changes without extensive memory overhead. The Composition API naturally encourages the extraction of complex reactive logic into reusable composables (functions starting with 'use'), providing a more structured and manageable way to build expansive, complex frontend applications while maintaining high levels of type safety. This architectural shift significantly improves the readability of large component files, simplifies dependency management between component chunks, and allows for cleaner, more scalable state management across the entire application interface.
slides.svgContent: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 400 200'><rect width='100%' height='100%' fill='#f0fdf4'/><circle cx='100' cy='100' r='40' fill='#4ade80' fill-opacity='0.5'/><circle cx='300' cy='100' r='40' fill='#4ade80' fill-opacity='0.5'/><path d='M140 100 H260' stroke='#166534' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#arrow)'/><text x='200' y='180' text-anchor='middle' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='#064e3b'>Modular Reactive Logic (Composition API)</text></svg>
studentPrompts: Contrast the Options API with the Composition API in terms of logic reusability.
How do `ref` and `reactive` handle reactivity differently under the hood?
Explain the importance of creating composables for reusable component logic.

slides.title: State Management & Cross-Component Communication
slides.content: Managing state effectively is paramount in modern frontend applications to ensure synchronization across disparate UI regions as the application grows in complexity. Whether leveraging Vue's reactive `provide`/`inject` for hierarchical communication, utilizing specialized libraries, or adopting React's context/hook-driven patterns, the fundamental requirement is a clean, immutable, and predictable data flow. Effective state orchestration ensures that UI updates only occur when absolutely necessary, minimizing unnecessary re-renders. A well-designed state architecture encapsulates volatile application state, separating it from stable view or UI state, which drastically improves testability and predictably simplifies component interaction. By enforcing unidirectional data flow, managing side effects outside of the view layer, and clearly defining the responsibilities of different state slices, developers can build robust, highly maintainable frontend architectures. This phase explores the architectural patterns required to orchestrate these state transitions efficiently across a single-page dashboard environment, balancing simplicity and scalability.

studentPrompts: What are the trade-offs of using `provide`/`inject` versus a centralized store like Pinia/Vuex?
Explain why unidirectional data flow is critical for complex applications.
How do you effectively separate volatile application-wide data from view-local state?
